We have recommended and management agreed that the Inventory
Committee created for the one-time cleansing of the PPE account
balances prepare a Physical Inventory Plan and execute the Plan in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We have recommended and management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant collate and consolidate all capitalizable expenditures
related to each structure/building or infrastructure asset, including
multi-year acquisitions and improvements. A comprehensive
depreciation schedule should then be prepared for each PPE item
based on the accumulated cost reflected in the Subsidiary Ledger,
taking into account the nature of improvements and any replaced or
derecognized components. The accountant should also determine or
revise the useful life of the assets, in consultation with the engineering
office or relevant technical personnel, to ensure accurate depreciation.
Subsequently, a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) should be prepared to
recognize the appropriate depreciation expense and accumulated
depreciation for prior and current periods in accordance with Manual on
the NGAS.

a) Conduct a complete physical inventory of all supplies and
materials in accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual
and prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of
Inventories (RPCI);
b) Ensure the regular maintenance of Stock Cards by the General
Services Office and Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs) by the
Accounting Unit as required under Sections 114 and 116 of the
NGAS Manual;
c) Require the use and proper documentation of Requisition and
Issue Slips (RIS) and the Summary of Supplies and Materials
Issued (SSMI) to support all issuances; and
d) Cease the practice of recording lump-sum inventory adjustments
without adequate basis and ensure that all accounting entries
are supported by complete and verifiable documents, in
compliance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

a) Require the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular and
systematic monitoring, verification, and validation of all fund
transfers to barangays, including issuing certifications
confirming liquidation of previous transfers before releasing
additional funds.

b) Furthermore, the Municipality promptly issue demand letters to
barangays with unliquidated balances and require the timely
submission of complete fund utilization reports to enforce
accountability and ensure transparency in the use of public
funds.

We have recommended and management agreed that management
coordinate with DOST-CAR to address the project implementation
issues and determine the appropriate course of action. Additionally,
management ensure strict compliance with the provisions of the
Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) by submitting timely requests for
project extensions, when necessary, to avoid implementation lapses
and to safeguard the delivery of the intended benefits to the target
beneficiaries.

We have recommended and management agreed that ensure
compliance with the Philippine Bidding Documents (PBD) and
Instructions to Bidders (ITB) to avoid inconsistencies in contract
implementation. Specifically, the grant of time extensions must be
consistent with the original contract terms using calendar days.

We recommended and management agreed that the Bids and Awards
Committee:

a) Rate the Single Largest Completed Contract submitted by
bidders as “passed” only if it is supported by an Owner’s
Certificate of Final Acceptance issued by the project owner other
than the contractor or a final rating of at least Satisfactory in the
Constructors Performance Evaluation System in accordance
with Section 23.4.2 of the IRR of RA No. 9184; and
b) Submit a written justification for the noted lapses in the
preliminary examination of bids.

a) Formally coordinate with the DA-NMIS to determine the current
status of the slaughterhouse project previously initiated in CY
2019, including whether the contractor has been officially
terminated, the extent of work completed, and the possibility of
either resuming the project. This will enable the Municipality to
decide on the most appropriate course of action whether to
continue the existing project in coordination with DA-NMIS or to
pursue a new slaughterhouse project independently, ensuring
that the needs of the constituents are adequately addressed.

b) Expedite the procurement and implementation of all other
unimplemented projects to avoid further delays. Additionally,
establish proper monitoring mechanisms to ensure the timely
execution and full utilization of the 20% Development Fund in
accordance with planning and budgeting priorities.

We have recommended and management agreed to direct the Building
Official to ensure the timely preparation of the Program of Works (POW)
covering the 15% share of OBO fund collections, including those from
CY 2024 and CY 2013–2023, for approval by the Local Chief Executive
(LCE). Furthermore, Management should require the immediate
submission of the approved POWs to the Department of Public Works
and Highways (DPWH), in strict compliance with the provisions of the
DPWH-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC). This will help ensure
the proper utilization of funds for their intended purposes and promote
adherence to applicable laws, rules, and regulations.

a) require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the un-
submitted disbursement vouchers totaling ₱596,544.70 including
its complete supporting documents; and

b) Identify and address the root causes of the delays and/or non-
submission of Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) and their
supporting documents, and ensure the submission of the same
within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month.

a) The Municipal Treasurer strictly enforce the daily remittance of
collections by the Revenue Collection Clerks (RCCs) to the
designated Liquidating Officer (LO). The RCCs should prepare
and submit the Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD), along
with the corresponding duplicate official receipts, at the close of
each business day. For field collectors who are stationed in
areas where daily travel to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office is
impractical, collections must be remitted at least once a week or
immediately upon reaching a total of ₱5,000.00, whichever
comes first.

b) The Liquidating Officer must diligently verify all collections and
accountable forms submitted by the RCCs and ensure that all
cash received is turned over intact to the Municipal Treasurer. In
turn, the Treasurer must deposit the collections into the
authorized depository bank on the same day they are received,
or no later than the next banking day, in full compliance with
Section 32 of COA Circular No. 92-382.

We commended the management of the Municipality for diligently
complying with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC Nos.
2016-01 and 2013-01, in the preparation and implementation of the GAD
Plan and Budget and the preparation and submission of the required
GAD Accomplishment Report.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) ensure that the:
Municipal Accountant:
Prepare the necessary adjusting entry; and
Strictly adhere to the accounting principles with reference to account title descriptions in recording transactions to their proper accounts, regardless of the budgetary charging of the expenditure.
Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) coordinates with the Municipal Accountant in the preparation of annual budget and in assessing whether the projects are under the appropriate classification.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to ensure that the:
Municipal Treasurer (MT) adhere to the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular 2012-002 particularly the transfer of unspent allotment of Current Year LDRMM Fund to its bank account at year-end;
Municipal Accountant to carefully monitor the balances per ledgers, schedules and reports and reconcile the balance of the Trust Liability-DRRM with the Cash-in Bank 5% Calamity fund once the actual cash was transferred by the MT to the LDRRM bank account to establish a sufficient cash back-up for the said trust account. We also require reconciliation amongst accounts. A JEV be prepared in recording the adjusting/correcting entries and furnished a copy of which to COA for reference; and
MBO to coordinate with the LFC in the monitoring and reconciliation of LDRRM budget, cash availability and financial statement account balances to determine amount still not fully utilized after five years  be reverted back to the GF.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed to ensure that the Municipal Accountant:
Perform a detailed review and analysis of the affected accounts and prepare the necessary correcting JEVs to have a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements;
Perform reconciliation with those agencies where over-remittances were made to ensure that only what is due to them will be remitted; and
For the over-remittance, prepare an analysis and/or monitor payroll deductions versus remittances to identify whether there exist over or under payment, for appropriate action.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to:
Instruct and ensure that the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) immediately update in the GSIS portal the salary details of employees;
Ensure that the Municipal Accountant and HRMO confer with GSIS on the reconciliation of unpaid premiums per individual accounts; and
Confer with the GSIS management and the LGU employees on the best resolution for the unpaid premiums.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed to ensure that the Municipal Accountant:
Observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the FS and its accompanying notes;
Prioritize attendance to specific training for/on SCBAA, Statement of Cash Flows, and other accounting guidelines for the staff handling; and,
Revisit the provisions of the IPSAS and other COA issuances, particularly on the SCBAA and the Statement of Financial Performance and the Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/ (Deficit).

We recommended that the LCE:
Ensure that the AOs refund the unremitted and undeposited collections immediately;
Ensure that the MT cause the withholding of the AO’s salaries, benefits and/or other emoluments, if payment is not made immediately; and
Cause for the relief of the AOs responsible for the shortages, without prejudice to the filing of civil, criminal, or administrative cases, as applicable.
We also recommended that the MT:
Ensure the withholding of the AO’s salaries, benefits and/or other emoluments, if payment is not made immediately; and
Recommend to the LCE qualified persons for designation as LOs and collectors.

We recommended, and Management agreed that the:
Collectors, LOs, and MT review and verify the correctness of the RCDs, and ensure that these are properly filled-up before submitting the same to the Audit Team to properly serve its purpose by providing the needed accounting and financial information to prospective users at any given point in time;
LO:
Verify correctness and agreement of the actual amounts remitted vis a vis the submitted RCD, otherwise return the RCD and request the Collector to revise the same to tally with the actual amount on hand for liquidation; and
Ensure that the ending balances of the RCDs was carried forward to the subsequent RCD.

We recommended that the LCE:
Hire an HRMO and create policy guidelines in the hiring of personnel under job order/contractofservices in accordance with Civil Service Commission (CSC), Revised Omnibus Rules, COA and other existing labor laws, rules and regulations, to give the workers, both regular and casual/contractual/job order, the benefits due them, define their accountabilities and contribute to the overall productivity of the Municipality Government; and
Instruct, monitor, and ensure that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, Municipal HRMO and MBO set plans and come up with an Organizational Structure such that all vacant positions are filled-up instead of resorting to Job Order/Contact of Service scheme and must include in their personnel schedule those appointed as co-terminus, contractual and contract of service.
Further, we also recommended that if merited by applicable CSC regulations, the HRMO evaluate various positions Job Order/Contract of Service personnel for possible appointment to permanent regular positions.

We recommended that the MBO obligate the:
Remaining contract value of infrastructure projects without obligation and make necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of unobligated allotment as of December 31, 2024;
Contracts/POs in full amount after perfection of the contracts/POs for all infrastructure projects and goods, and not every time payments are processed; and
Multi-year contracts in proportion of the approved appropriation of the current year.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to facilitate the conduct of the one-time cleansing of PPE by performing the following:
Identify other departments within the LGU that may have available staff with relevant skills (e.g., record-keeping, accounting, or logistics) who can be temporarily reassigned to assist and focus with the inventory process.
Collaborate with other government agencies that can provide training on inventory management, especially on the full implementation of the 'One-time Cleansing of PPE Accounts,' as outlined in COA Circular 2020-006, so that the LGU may be fully aware of the primary guidelines of the said circular.
Establish a new set of Inventory Committee, with members temporarily relieved of their regular duties until the completion of the physical inventory within a clear and agreed-upon timeline.
Ensure that the newly reconstituted Inventory Committee prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the COA Audit Team at least ten calendar days before the scheduled start of the inventory-taking process.

We recommended that the LSB:
Prepare and submit SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1 as provided in Annex A of JC No. 1 s. 2017;
Obtain SEF previous years unexpendedbalances including continuing appropriation to determine the net amount of available for appropriation; and
Present the SEF Budget by P/A/P by allotment class and per school using SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Accountant be directed to thoroughly review past transactions, reconcile the reciprocal accounts, and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances in order that reliable data be presented. A copy of the JEVs taking up the adjustment shall be furnished to the office of the Auditor.

We recommended and the management agreed to:

Establish hiring policies/guidelines and criteria (designation, function, qualification, specific/ lump sum/intermittent work/job and services, etc.); and

Evaluate the staffing pattern of the municipality to fill up the vacant and funded positions of the LGU in order to limit the hiring of contract orders and job order employees and be guided by the pertinent provisions of the Civil Service Commission.

We recommended that the LCE directs the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to reconcile their records in order to come up with a more reliable costing of the PPE accounts and the Municipal Accountant to draw a journal entry voucher to adjust/correct the affected accounts if necessary.

We recommended that the accountable officer faithfully adheres to the existing rules and regulations on the recording and reporting of the receipts and payments of all cash advances handled by her to facilitate monitoring and verification thereof, prevent loss or misuse of government funds and avoid possible shortage in audit, which has penal sanctions.

We recommended that:

The LDRRMC prepares and submits the updated LDRRMFIP to the LDC.



The LDC is to prepare and submit approved updated AIP.

We recommended that management:

(a) Prepare the CapDev Agenda in accordance with the above-cited regulations; and 

(b) Ensure that the training, seminars, and other related activities attended and/or conducted are in accordance with the CapDev Agenda to adequately address the competency gaps of the personnel and the participants.

We recommended that the General Service Officer prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report of Official Travel and Fuel Consumption supported by all the Driver's Trip Tickets to provide the Team with adequate documentation in evaluating the regularity and reasonableness of the fuel consumption incurred by the Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management coordinates with the PhilHealth Regional Offices (PROs) within their respective catchment areas to cover government-hired JOs and PBCs under the program. Also, submit a justification for non-compliance with the program of PhilHealth.

We recommended that management refrains from authorizing deductions from the employees' salaries that will reduce their net take-home pay below 5,000.00 in CY 2021. Deductions from salaries should be prioritized as detailed in the GAA.

We recommended that the Management prepare and submit the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed, and Balances with supporting documents to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC), copy furnished to the Audit Team for all donations received, COVID-19 or non-COVID-19 related supplies and equipment from the private companies in the calendar year 2021 and every quarter thereafter if there is any.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strengthen cash transactions' efficiency and effectiveness by implementing effective internal control. 

The Municipal Treasure must observe the following: 

1. The Municipal Treasurer/Liquidating Officer must record the collections and deposits daily in the cash book, 

2 Collections must be deposited intact and as frequently as required by regulations, 

3. The cashbook balance must be reconciled with the cash on hand at the close of each day, 

4. Cashbook balances must be reconciled with the subsidiary ledgers at least once every quarter, 

5. The Accountable Form Custodian must adequately control the ordering, storing, issuing, and recording of unused official receipts and other accountable forms, 

6. A responsible officer of the agency must closely supervise the collecting officers.

We recommended, in cases where the conditions provided for in the COA Circular No. 2016-005 are met, the Local Chief Executive shall file the request for authority to write-off the dormant unliquidated cash advances to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and Supervising Auditor (SA) following the procedures and specific guidelines set forth in Paragraph 8.0 of the said Circular.

We recommended that the General Services Officer (GSO) prepare the Report on the Local Road Network and Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System with the Inventory Committee's help. 

Furthermore, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain the subsidiary ledger for roads and their components, reconcile it with the result of the physical count at year-end, correctly depreciate the accounts, and disclose it in the Notes to the Financial Statements as prescribed.

In line with this, the management shall:

We recommended and the management agreed that they require the immediate liquidation of the said cash advances. The accountable officers should also faithfully adhere to the existing rules and regulations on the granting, utilization, and liquidation of all cash advances handled by them to facilitate monitoring and verification thereof, prevent loss and/or misuse of government funds, and avoid possible shortage in audit, which has penal sanctions. Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials abide COA circular 97-002.

We also recommended that the Management install sound internal control to deter the possible occurrence of misuse of government funds.
We recommended and the management agreed to stop the practice of paying their supplier or any payee in advance from the undeposited collection of the Municipal Treasury Office.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Accounting Office:

Apply the criteria on capitalization of PPE as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2022-004 to ensure proper classification of assets in the books; and

Make the necessary adjustments to correct and/or restate the balances of affected accounts.

Also, the GSO Designate:

Provide data to the Accounting Office relevant to items reclassified as semi-expendable property including information on their condition. 

Update existing PPE and Inventory records and prepare and maintain the additional necessary forms/reports/registries for purposes of record-keeping and installation of controls over semi-expendable properties provided in Section 4 of the Circular; and

Onwards, use the Inventory Custodian’s Slip (ICS) for issuance of semi-expendable items to establish monitoring, control, and accountability over them.

We recommended and the management agreed that they requires:

The GSO to conduct an inventory count of these various Covid-19 vaccines, drugs, medicines and other medical supplies received to determine what is left of these inventories;

the MHO to inform the accounting of any receipt of medical supplies and inventory from any sources and forward the documents as proof of receipt by the municipality to be the basis for recording in the book of accounts; and

the Municipal Accountant to record the Inventories of medical supplies and medicines still available at the end of the year 2022 in the book of accounts of the LGU.

We recommended and the management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant be directed to (a) prepare the necessary adjusting JEVs to reclassify the accounts, and (b) ensure that the transactions are recorded in an appropriate account classification.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Accountant be directed to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the Loans Payable – Domestic account to provide clarity and divulge information that is not readily available on the face of the financial statements as prescribed in IPSAS No. 1.

We recommended and the management agreed that the LCE requires the Municipal Accountant to prepare to adjust entries both in the general fund and trust fund books to correct the recording of the subject accounts.

We recommended and the management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant be directed to (a) prepare the necessary adjusting JEVs to reclassify the accounts, and (b) establish a mechanism to ensure the transactions are recorded in an appropriate account classification.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Accountant be directed to take up the money value of the earned leave credits of the officers and employees of the municipality to have a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the management agreed that the LCE requires that:

the BAC and the Municipal Engineer ensure that all projects which are unclear or presented in lump sum have the proper approval of the Sanggunian before the start of the procurement process and the implementation of the project; and

the Municipal Planning Officer to ensure that the projects to be implemented and listed in the Annual Investment Program (AIP) should be with sufficient detail, the project to be done is clear, and the budget is not presented in a lump sum.

We recommended and the management agreed that the LCE gives sufficient time in the planning, preparation, and deliberation of the development projects to be recommended to the Sangguniang Bayan which will be incorporated into the Annual Budget of the Municipality.

Also, as much as possible, the procurement of the projects must be based on the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) prepared by the Bids and Awards Committee through the BAC Secretariat in order to attain the prompt implementation of the projects.

We recommended and the management agreed that the LCE requires the Municipal Engineer to regularly and appropriately monitor the implementation of the projects under the 20% fund and ensure that all projects implemented are included in the quarterly utilization report.

We recommended and the management agreed that the LCE:

Require the Municipal Accountant to make sure that all documents required under Item 9, especially Item 9.1.1, of COA circular 2012-001 are attached to the disbursement voucher; and

Require the Municipal Engineer to submit a request for inspection to COA within a reasonable time after the completion of the project.

We recommended that the General Services Officer (GSO) prepare the Report on Local Road Network and Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System with the Inventory Committee's help. 
Furthermore, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain the subsidiary ledger for roads and their components, reconcile it with the result of the physical count at year-end, correctly depreciate the accounts, and disclose it in the Notes to the Financial Statements as prescribed.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive be compliant with the general guidelines, reporting and monitoring the information and publicity on Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) as imposed in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 31, 2013. 

In line with this, the management shall: 

a. Submit to the Audit Team the list of all on-going government Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented during the year,

 b. Inform this Office within 10 days after the infrastructure project's award or before starting the program/ activity that the appropriate project signboards and public notices are already posted,

 c. And submit to this Office the monthly monitoring report on PPAs.

We recommended that the GSO be directed to strictly comply with the above-cited regulations by renewing the PARs of the PPEs every three (3) years or as soon as there is a change in custodian or user. 

Strengthen control and accountability over government assets by informing the GSO whenever there is a change in the custodian or user of the PPE items from each department.
We recommended that the municipality demand for the payment of the loans receivables; and likewise, demand for the liquidation of the unliquidated financial assistance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal Accountant to 

(a) perform a detailed review and analysis of the affected accounts and prepare the necessary correcting journal entry vouchers to have a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements; and 

(b) strengthen accounting controls by performing a regular review on the journal entry and posting in the ledgers made by the accounting staff.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to regularly reconcile the balances of reciprocal accounts in the financial statements. 

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant make a detailed review of the accounts and determine the cause of the unreconciled amounts and make the necessary corrections in the books of accounts to present the corrected balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations in the preparation of the financial statements. 

Also, require the Municipal Accountant to revisit the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) provisions and other COA issuances, particularly on preparing and presenting accounts in the financial statements.

We reiterate our recommendation for the management to:

a. Institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex- disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation; and

b. Undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the PPAs are responsive to the said issues.

We recommended that management to strictly comply with the schedules to be observed in GAD planning and budgeting to facilitate the timely submission of the GAD Plan and Budget to the PPDO and DILG for review and endorsement.
We recommended that management to ensure compliance with the provisions of CSC resolutions to ensure protection of government funds from unnecessary expenses; and
We recommended that management to ensure that all accountable officers are adequately bonded to protect the interest of the government pursuant to applicable laws and regulations

We recommended that Management and the Council to formulate/adopt a comprehensive plan on delinquency prevention which includes the following:
a. Local State of Children’s Report

We recommended that Management to insure the motor vehicles with the GSIS and register with the LTO to avoid additional expenses in terms of penalties and payment of damages for third party liabilities, in case of vehicular accidents.

We recommended that the LCE directs the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to reconcile their records in order to come up with more reliable costing of the PPE accounts; and moreover, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant be directed to draw a journal entry voucher to adjust/correct the affected accounts if necessary.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Treasurer update the cashbooks regularly, and;
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer identify the discrepancies between the ledger balances and cashbook balances and effect the necessary adjustments in their records to arrive at reconciled balances.
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer conduct periodic reconciliation of balances between the accounting’s ledgers and treasury’s cashbooks at least quarterly.

We recommended that the GSO conduct an inventory of these supplies and equipment received to determine what is left of these supplies and the actual existence of the equipment and for the issuance of PAR to the Accountable Officers for this equipment. In addition, the MHO is to forward to the accounting office any documents received relative to these supplies and equipment. Finally, the OIC-Accountant records these supplies and equipment in the books of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to take up the money value of the earned leave credits of the officers and employees of the municipality to have a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, OIC-Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Treasurer be directed to submit the paid disbursement vouchers, including supporting documents, to the audit team for examination and verification purposes in compliance with the above-cited regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer/Cashier/Accountant be directed to submit the transaction documents such as Disbursement Vouchers, Payrolls, Official Receipts, JEVs for adjustments, liquidations, etc. and their corresponding supporting documents within the reglementary period to ensure timely evaluation of same and deficiencies or other observations noted should there be any will also be immediately corrected or adjusted.

We recommended that management:
Strictly follow the prescribed frequency of liquidation/remittance by remitting intact the accountable officer’s collections promptly and regularly, especially during Fridays or days prior to long holidays, to mitigate the risk of loss, theft, and misuse of government funds.
Avoid issuing new stubs of accountable forms to collectors that have an unliquidated/unremitted collection.

We recommended that the accountable officer faithfully adheres to the existing rules and regulations on the recording and reporting of the receipts and payments of all cash advances handled by her to facilitate monitoring and verification thereof, prevent loss or misuse of government funds and avoid possible shortage in audit, which has penal sanctions.

We recommended the management require all the collectors to faithfully adhere to the existing rules and regulations on the recording of the receipt and remittance of all collections handled by them to facilitate monitoring and verification thereof, prevent loss and/or misuse of government funds and avoid possible shortage in audit, which has penal sanctions.

We recommended to management to require all the accountable officers to faithfully adhere to the existing rules and regulations on the receipt and remittance/deposit of all collections handled by them to prevent loss and/or misuse of government funds and avoid possible shortage in audit, which have penal sanctions.
Require also the Municipal Treasurer to properly monitor all collectors, as this is one of the major functions of his office, so that income or collections of the Municipality would be correctly receipted, accounted and recorded in the books and properly reported in the period in which they were earned.

We recommended that:
the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile the Treasurer’s Cashbook with the accounting records, at least quarterly, as mandated by the Revised Cash Examination Manual.
Prepare the necessary adjustment/s for the P110,989.14 negative balance in the Treasurer’s Cashbook.

We recommended that:
The LDRRMC prepares and submit the updated LDRRMFIP to the LDC.
The LDC to prepare and submit approved updated AIP.
Moving forward, the Management be observant and strictly abide by prescribed laws, rules, and regulations in making revisions of the Annual Budget and Annual Investment Programs.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that:
The Inventory Committee shall assign a unique Property Number for each PPE item following the required code format in the numbering system as provided in Section 5 of the Circular.
The Inventory Committee shall prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and submit the same to the COA Audit Team at least ten calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities in compliance with the pertinent provisions of Section 5 of the Circular.
Each office of the LGU shall fill up the Inventory Count Form and submit the same to the Property Unit for verification. The form will then be used by the Inventory Committee as a basis in preparing its PIP and will be considered part of its inventory working paper.
Moreover, the Inventory Committee shall record/document the physical count daily using the ICF per PPE sub-major account group and will be the basis in the preparation of RPCPPE after the physical count.
The Property Unit shall prepare a List of PPEs Found at Station (Annex B of the Circular) and List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs (Annex C of the Circular) during physical inventory and submit these lists to the Accounting Unit for recording in the books of accounts of the LGU to establish the existence and proper accountability of the PPE accounts.
The LCE shall ensure that the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of PPE as provided in COA Circular 2020-006 are strictly observed to facilitate the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.

We recommended the LCE should:
Create an Inventory Committee with an adequate number of members and representatives from the Accounting and Property Division/Unit;
Instruct the Inventory Committee to:
prioritize the completion of physical count of PPEs; and
prepare the RPCPPE, reconcile the same with the Property Division/Unit and Accounting Unit records
Instruct the Accounting Unit to prepare the necessary adjusting entries, if any, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the responsible Municipal Officials to immediately submit the pertinent documents of the above projects to facilitate the technical review and inspections, and moving forward, strictly comply with the above-mentioned regulations. Also, the management to identify the cause of non-submission and take necessary action to solve the issue.

We recommended that management:
(a) Prepare the CapDev Agenda in accordance with the above-cited regulations; and
(b) Ensure that the training, seminars, and other related activities attended and/or conducted are in accordance with the CapDev Agenda to adequately address the competency gaps of the personnel and the participants.

We reiterated our recommendation from our previous AOM that the municipality should prioritize the processing of the titling of the lands to prove claim of ownership and to safeguard government interest.

We recommended that the General Service Officer prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Fuel Consumption supported by all the Driver's Trip Ticket to provide the Team with adequate documentation in evaluating the regularity and reasonableness of the fuel consumption incurred by the Municipality.
Also, before effecting payment on the claims, the Accountant should see to it that all supporting documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers duly provided with relevant details.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management coordinates with the PhilHealth Regional Offices (PROs) within their respective catchment areas to cover government-hired JOs and PBCs under the program. Also, to submit justification for non-compliance with the program of PhilHealth.

We recommended that management refrain from authorizing deductions from the employees' salaries that will reduce their net take-home pay below 5,000.00 in CY 2021. Deductions from salaries should be prioritized as detailed in the GAA.

We recommended that the Municipal General Services Officer prepare the requirements for all the unserviceable office furniture and equipment so that disposal of the same could be affected and in order to avail of higher economic value when offered in a public auction. Also, the management to undertake the immediate disposal of the unserviceable office furniture and equipment. Upon disposal, the Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher to drop the value of unserviceable properties from the books.

We recommended that the Management prepare and submit the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances with supporting documents to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC), copy furnished the Audit Team for all donations received, COVID-19 or non-COVID-19 related supplies and equipment from the private companies in the calendar year 2021 and every quarter thereafter if there is any.
We recommended that the Provincial Accountant effect the necessary adjustments to present fairly the balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed that:

Copies of Acceptance and Inspection Reports of procured or donated inventory items be immediately furnished to the Accounting Division for recognition of the transactions in the books; and

We recommended and Management agreed that all required reports and relevant information on programs, projects and activities of the agency be submitted to the Audit Team, within timelines set, to enable the publication of updated project status and to promote good governance through transparency and accountability.

We recommended that the General Services Officer facilitate the immediate renewal of registration of the nine motor vehicles with the LTO.  Any penalties imposed due to delinquent renewal of registration shall be personally borne by the accountable officer.

We recommended and Management agreed that the GSO initiate the process of insuring the motor vehicles by registering with the LTO the vehicles under the name of the Provincial Government and by accomplishing and submitting the Property Insurance Form to the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS.

We recommended and Management agreed that prior to the commencement of construction projects, the necessary building permits be secured, and that any penalties incurred for non-adherence therewith shall be personally borne by the accountable officer.

We recommended that PPAs funded from the 20% DF be vetted to ensure that these are the priority needs and are implementable within the year to optimize use of resources and deliver the expected socio-economic development and environment outcomes to the general public.  We further recommended that re-allocations be made, as appropriate, to meet current priorities of the public.
We recommended that the Municipality comply with the provisions of the Local Budget Circular (LBC) 125 and submit the monthly report using the prescribed format (Annex C) to the auditor's office.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strengthen cash transactions' efficiency and effectiveness by implementing effective internal control. 

The Municipal Treasure must observe the following: 

1. There must be a clear-cut separation of the cashiering function from the accounting function, 

2. The Municipal Treasurer/Liquidating Officer must record the collections and deposits daily in the cash book, 

3. Collections must be deposited intact and as frequently as required by regulations, 

4. The cashbook balance must be reconciled with the cash on hand at the close of each day, 

5. Cashbook balances must be reconciled with the subsidiary ledgers at least once every quarter, 

6. Monthly reports of accountability for accountable forms must be prepared and submitted, 

7. The Accountable Form Custodian must adequately control the ordering, storing, issuing, and recording of unused official receipts and other accountable forms, 

8. The person who made a deposit must be escorted by armed guards and provided with transportation equipment, 

9. A responsible officer of the agency must closely supervise the collecting officers, 

10. The stock of unused checks must be accessible only to custodians and those who prepare and sign checks, 

11. Voided or spoiled checks must be marked cancelled and held available for subsequent inspection; and 

12. Cash advances of the Disbursing Officer must not exceed the actual amount to be disbursed.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant review prior years’ records and identify the payees for Management to notify them of the status of the checks and take other possible actions such as cancellation or replacement of the subject checks. 

Moreover, the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the cancellation of the stale checks.

We recommended the following: 
1. Strictly enforce the liquidation of all cash advances within thirty (30) days upon return to the permanent official station; 

2. Demand letter shall be made by the Municipal Accountant for the immediate liquidation and settlement of all cash advances outstanding as of December 31, 2020, from concerned officials and employees, including the suspension of salaries; and 

3. Strictly adhere to applicable rules and regulations in the grant, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the possibility of liquidation and or collection of the unliquidated cash advances and prepare the required quarterly report. 

Moreover, in cases where the conditions provided for in the COA Circular No. 2016-005 are met, the Local Chief Executive shall file the request for authority to write-off the dormant unliquidated cash advances to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and Supervising Auditor (SA) following the procedures and specific guidelines set forth in Paragraph 8.0 of the said Circular.

We recommended that management and the accountable officer to faithfully adhere to the existing rules and regulations on the granting, utilization, and liquidation of all cash advances to facilitate monitoring and verification and to prevent loss and/or misuse of government funds, and avoid possible shortage in audit, which has penal sanctions.

 We also recommended that management install sound internal control to deter the possible misuse of government funds.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to provide depreciation to the above PPEs and submit the lapsing schedule of depreciation for all PPEs.

We recommended that: 

1. The Municipal Mayor directs the office or personnel concerned to expedite the preparation of the Requisition Issue Slip and Fuel Consumption reports; 

2. The General Services Officer (GSO) properly monitor the movement of all inventories from the warehouse or any secured space and observed the required requisition procedure for proper issuance and recording of issued/distributed inventories; and 

3. The GSO prepares the monthly Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI), summarizing all the Requisition Issued Slip.

We recommended that the management discontinue accepting collection receipts or delivery receipts in place of the sales invoice or official receipts as proof of payment.

 On the other hand, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer demand from the supplier for the sales invoices or official receipts to obtain principal evidence on the payment of goods.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to reclassify the accounts in the financial statements. 

Moving forward, the Municipal Accountant be directed to establish a mechanism to ensure that the transactions are recorded in the appropriate account as prescribed in the Chart of Account for LGUs.

We recommended that the BAC or BAC Secretariat submit the contracts together with the documentary requirements enumerated in the checklists for submission to the COA Audit Team for the auditorial and legal review and ultimately to the COA Technical Audit Services depending on the amount involved.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to make the necessary correcting entries to correct the possible effects to the financial statements.

We recommended that Management to coordinate with the PhilHealth Regional Offices (PROs) within their respective catchment areas for the coverage of government hired JOs and PBCs under the program. Also, to submit justification for non-compliance with the program of PhilHealth.
Require the Budget Officer to include in the Budget the necessary funds for the premiums of properties to be insured with GSIS.

Recommendation
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strengthen cash transactions' efficiency and effectiveness by implementing effective internal control. The Municipal Treasure must observe the following: 1. There must be a clear-cut separation of the cashiering function from the accounting function, 2. The Municipal Treasurer/Liquidating Officer must record the collections and deposits daily in the cash book, 3. Collections must be deposited intact and as frequently as required by regulations, 4. The cashbook balance must be reconciled with the cash on hand at the close of each day, 5. Cashbook balances must be reconciled with the subsidiary ledgers at least once every quarter, 6. Monthly reports of accountability for accountable forms must be prepared and submitted, 7. The Accountable Form Custodian must adequately control the ordering, storing, issuing, and recording of unused official receipts and other accountable forms, 8. The person who made a deposit must be escorted by armed guards and provided with transportation equipment, 9. A responsible officer of the agency must closely supervise the collecting officers, 10. The stock of unused checks must be accessible only to custodians and those who prepare and sign checks, 11. Voided or spoiled checks must be marked cancelled and held available for subsequent inspection; and 12. Cash advances of the Disbursing Officer must not exceed the actual amount to be disbursed.

Recommendation
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant review prior years’ records and identify the payees for Management to notify them of the status of the checks and take other possible actions such as cancellation or replacement of the subject checks. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the cancellation of the stale checks.

We recommended the following: 1. Strictly enforce the liquidation of all cash advances within thirty (30) days upon return to the permanent official station; 2. Moreover, in cases where the conditions provided for in the COA Circular No. 2016-005 are met, the Local Chief Executive shall file the request for authority to write-off the dormant unliquidated cash advances to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and Supervising Auditor (SA) following the procedures and specific guidelines set forth in Paragraph 8.0 of the said Circular.

Moreover, in cases where the conditions provided for in the COA Circular No. 2016-005 are met, the Local Chief Executive shall file the request for authority to write-off the dormant receivable accounts, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfers to the  Audit Team Leader () and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) following the procedures and specific guidelines set forth in Paragraph 8.0 of the said Circular.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) directs the concerned Officers and/or employees to deposit amount with the Authorized Government Depository bank immediately.
Moving forward, we recommended that the LCE requires the accountable officer to strictly observe the rules and regulations pertaining to the collections and deposits of the government funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements. Also, direct the Municipal Accountant to revisit the provisions of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) and other COA issuances particularly on the preparation and presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal Engineering Office to immediately submit the pertinent documents of the above projects to facilitate the conduct of the technical review and/or inspections. Also, we recommend that management institute measures to ensure strict compliance with the above-mentioned regulations.

We recommended that management to: (a) prepare the CapDev Agenda in accordance with the above-cited regulations; and (b) ensure that the trainings, seminars and other related activities attended and/or conducted are in accordance with the CapDev Agenda to properly address the competency gaps of the personnel and the participants.

We recommended that: (a) the GSO strictly comply with the above-cited regulations by renewing the PARs of the PPEs every three (3) years or as soon as there is a change in custodian/user to strengthen control and accountability over government assets; and (b) the concerned Head of Office to inform the GSO whenever there is a change in the custodian and/or user of the PPE items in their respective offices.

We recommended that management: (a) institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation; and (b) Undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the PPAs are responsive to the said issues.
Moreover, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive to establish a monitoring and evaluation system by issuing an Executive Order creating the M&E Team and ensure that the team will function as required by the regulations.
We further recommended that the management to extent the monitoring and evaluation of the PPAs on the remaining 95% of the budget to increase the gender-responsiveness of the programs and projects.

We recommended that management strictly comply with the schedules to be observed in GAD planning and budgeting to facilitate the timely submission of the GAD Plan and Budget to the PPDO and DILG for review and endorsement.

We recommended that management comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 2013 -004 dated January 30, 2013 on Information and Publicity on PPA of government agencies, particularly on the posting of Signboards specified in Annex “A” and the general guidelines and reporting and monitoring. Also, to regularly update the status of the PPAs in the signboards.

Require the Municipal Accountant to send confirmation letters to identified payees through all possible means. Unconfirmed accounts and/or payees that could no longer be located after exhausting all administrative means, may be properly reverted to the Equity Account since no actual claim was filed, judicial or administrative, in pursuance to the provision of Section 98 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Treasurer to enforce collection of the delinquent real property taxes by conducting tax education campaign, otherwise avail of the remedies provided in Sections 256 to 260 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that management to establish mechanism to ensure compliance with the above-cited regulations. Also, to submit the required documents immediately to facilitate the technical review and inspection

We recommend that the management to ensure that the PPAs to be funded from the LDRRMF are in accordance with the guidelines provided in the above-mentioned JMC. 

Also, submit a written explanation/justification for utilizing the LDRRMF in the procurement of garbage truck

We recommended that the concerned accountable officer to affect the necessary adjustment in their respective records and to regularly adhere with the rules and regulation on the proper handling, custody and disposition of records especially on the reconciliation of the balances.

We recommended that Management to comply with the requirement of the above-cited regulations for the GFPS members to be guided on the GAD policies to ensure that the programs, projects and activities of the municipality will be GAD responsive. 

Also, to establish mechanism to ensure that the GFPS are performing their mandated functions.

We recommended that the Management to prioritize the implementation of the development projects so that the expected output/outcome of the projects will be attained.  

Also, to implement the projects by contract to avoid the difficulty in looking for the availability of the equipment.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to ensure compliance with the above-cited regulation before approving the project to be implemented by administration.

We recommended that Management and the Council to formulate/adopt a comprehensive plan on delinquency prevention which includes the following:
Local Code for Children
Local Development Plan for Children
Local Investment Plan for Children
Children’s Profile
Local State of Children’s Report and
The Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan.

We recommended that the Management to allocate one per cent (1%) of its Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for the implementation of the LCPC PPAs in line with the adopted child delinquency prevention plan.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to effect immediately all reconciling items in the respective records to put in order the balances of accounts in the financial statements. Conduct periodic reconciliation of records in the ensuing periods to ensure the reliability of records and in compliance with Section 74 of PD 1445.
Also, to require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately deposit all undeposited collections as of December 31, 2016 to preclude misuse or misappropriation of the same.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify tax collection efforts to generate additional income to finance projects beneficial to the constituents and management to take initiatives to finds ways and means in order to maximize the exercise of its revenue raising power.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should instruct the General Service Officer and the BAC Secretariat to see to it that all supporting documents are properly accomplished. 

Likewise, the Accounting Unit shall verify the completeness of the supporting documents before certifying and processing the claims against the funds of the LGU.
Management should inform the Treasurer to stop issuing checks without an approved disbursement vouchers or without an appropriation.

Require the Treasurer and other officials concerned to ensure all insurable risks to protect the interest of the Municipality. Also require the Budget Officer to include in the Budget the necessary funds for the premiums of properties to be insured with GSIS.

We recommend that Management require the Treasurer to find ways on how to facilitate the preparation and submission of the duly certified list showing the names of all taxpayers and the tax due from each taxpayer at the start of the year to the Accountant as a basis of recording in the books of accounts the RPT/SET Receivables. This way, the actual RPT/SET Receivables every beginning of each year is reflected in the books of accounts. 

Moreover, the Accountant should make a detailed analysis of the Receivable accounts and their contra accounts to determine the cause/s of the discrepancies and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management to strengthen accounting controls over liabilities by requiring the Municipal Accountant to review thoroughly the schedule of payables due to the concerned agencies, and to reconcile the account and effect necessary adjustments or correction to be reflected in the books of the LGU.

We recommend that Demand Letters be issued to the erring officials and employees of the municipality but this time a reasonable liquidation period be set whereby the appropriate charges shall then be filed if left unattended pursuant to Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code. Civil actions to be initiated to run after their properties. For those still in the active service the corresponding withholding from benefits due them shall be made to apply to the unliquidated cash advances.
Henceforth, the granting of cash advances shall be in accordance with of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997

The Municipal Accountant shall:
Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of the receivables, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfers, and determine the concerned debtors, and the source and implementing government entities concerned;  and
Reconcile the unliquidated fund transfers between the sources and implementing government entities, prepare the adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted, and require liquidation of balances.
In cases where the conditions provided for in the COA Circular No. 2016-005 are present, the Local Chief Executive may file the request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfers to the  Audit Team Leader () and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) following the procedures and specific guidelines set forth in the Circular.

Require the Municipal Accountant to send confirmation letters to identified payees through all possible means. Unconfirmed accounts and/or payees that could no longer be located after exhausting all administrative means, may be properly reverted to the Equity Account since no actual claim was filed, judicial or administrative, in pursuance to the provision of Section 98 of PD 1445.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the following suggested measures:
Deposit all collections intact with the authorized depository bank as often as necessary; and
Stop immediately the practice of holding large amount of undeposited collections.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly review past transactions, reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances in order that a reliable data is presented. A copy of the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) taking up the adjustment shall be furnished the office of the Auditor.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly review past transactions, reconcile the accounts and to determine the causes of the differences. Any adjustment noted in the process shall be effected by drawing the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reflect the correct balances of the accounts.

We recommend that the Municipal General Services Officer prepare the requirements for all the unserviceable office furniture and equipment so that disposal of the same could be effected and in order to avail of higher economic value when offered in public auction.
Also, the management to undertake the immediate disposal of the unserviceable office furniture and equipment and upon disposal, the Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher to drop from the books the value of unserviceable properties.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Treasurer to enforce collection of the delinquent real property taxes by conducting tax education campaign, otherwise avail of the remedies provided in Sections 256 to 260 of RA No. 7160.

We recommend that the Management to prioritize the implementation of the development projects so that the expected output/outcome of the projects will be attained. Further, for management to re-consider the implementation of projects by Administration in relation to its capability to fast track the implementation of these projects.

We recommend that management to:
Evaluate the staffing pattern of the local government and consider the creation of the separate GSO with staff/personnel to perform the mandated functions of the GSO; and
Require the GSO or designated personnel to submit a copy of the contracts and purchase orders within the prescribed period from issuance together with the relevant documents required under COA Circular 2009-001.

We recommend that management to establish mechanism to ensure compliance with the above-cited regulations. Also, to submit the required documents immediately to facilitate the technical review and inspection.

We recommend that the management to:
Require the LDRRMO to prioritize programs/projects/activities to be funded from the LDRRMF to ensure the readiness of the Municipality in case of calamity;
To ensure that the PPAs will be implemented in an efficient and effective manner to avoid the conduct of similar activities every budget year; and
To coordinate with other agencies providing evacuation facilities for the latter to be equipped with the necessary amenities.

We recommend that the management to ensure that the PPAs to be funded from the LDRRMF are in accordance with the guidelines provided in the above-mentioned JMC. Also, submit a written explanation/justification for utilizing the LDRRMF in the procurement of garbage truck.

We recommend that management to:
Conduct thorough evaluation and review of the municipality’s need for hiring casuals, contractual and JO employees;
Ensure compliance with the provisions of CSC resolutions to ensure protection of government funds from unnecessary expenses; and
Require the HRMO to evaluate the staffing pattern of the Municipality in order to limit the hiring of casual, contractual and job order employees and be guided by the provisions of the CSC resolutions.

We recommend that management to comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 2013 -004 dated January 30, 2013 on Information and Publicity on PPA of government agencies, particularly on the posting of Signboards specified in Annex “A” and the general guidelines, and reporting and monitoring.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer to stop effecting the changes/adjustment in the cashbook retrospectively, instead effect the necessary adjustment in the date when it was detected/occurred. Also, to update the cashbook daily in accordance with the above-mentioned rules and regulations.

We recommend that the concerned accountable officer to effect the necessary adjustment in their respective records and to regularly adhere with the rules and regulation on the proper handling, custody and disposition of records especially on the reconciliation of the balances.
We recommend that management to ensure that all accountable officers are adequately bonded to protect the interest of the government pursuant to applicable laws and regulations.

We recommend that the GAD Focal Point System to:
Institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation;
Undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the PPAs are responsive to the said issues; and
Revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, which identifies the data requirements for particular gender issues.

We recommend that Management to comply with the requirement of the above-cited regulations for the GFPS members to be guided on the GAD policies to ensure that the programs, projects and activities of the municipality will be GAD responsive. Also, to establish mechanism to ensure that the GFPS are performing their mandated functions.

We recommend that Management to coordinate with the PhilHealth Regional Offices (PROs) within their respective catchment areas for the coverage of government hired JOs and PBCs under the program. Also, to submit justification for non-compliance with the program of PhilHealth.

Moreover, the GSO to issue the ARE/PAR for the three (3) laptops and ensure that all issuances of property to the end user will be covered by an ARE/PAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management to require the Municipal Accountant to effect the adjustment of the above-mentioned transactions in the books of accounts to have a fair presentation in the financial statements. Also, the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all the transactions will be properly taken-up in the books of accounts. Moreover, the GSO to issue the ARE/PAR for the three (3) laptops and ensure that all issuances of property to the end user will be covered by an ARE/PAR.

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer to provide appropriations for the expense accounts which are among the reconciling items. On the other hand, the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary Journal Entry Vouchers to take-up the above-mentioned reconciling items in the books of accounts.

We recommend that Management require the Treasurer to find ways on how to facilitate the preparation and submission of the duly certified list showing the names of all taxpayers and the tax due from each taxpayer at the start of the year to the Accountant as a basis of recording in the books of accounts the RPT/SET Receivables. This way, the actual RPT/SET Receivables every beginning of each year is reflected in the books of accounts. Moreover, the Accountant should make a detailed analysis of the Receivable accounts and their contra accounts to determine the cause/s of the discrepancies and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher to take-up the transactions in the books of account.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to prepare the adjusting Journal Entry Voucher to correct the recording of the transactions in the books of account.

We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Determine the legitimacy of the transactions, and if found to be with valid claims, communicate with the concerned payees for the payment/settlement of the accounts;
Revert to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund those that proved to be with no legal claims; and
Conduct periodic review and monitoring of the Accounts Payable so that this account will consist only of valid claims and any erroneous recording will be corrected/adjusted immediately.

We recommend that Management to prioritize the implementation of these projects. Also, to implement the projects by contract to avoid the difficulty in looking for the available equipment. Moreover, to assess their capability before deciding on the mode of implementation of the PPAs to avoid government funds from being idle for a certain period of time.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to require the contractor and the Municipal Engineer to submit a written explanation/justification on the above-noted delay in the implementation of the projects. Also, to consider the imposition of the appropriate remedies/sanctions provided in the above-cited regulation against the contractor.

We recommend that Management require the LDRRMO to prioritize and implement the programs/projects/activities identified during the year to ensure the readiness of the Municipality in case of calamity. Also, to align the PPAs to be funded out of 70% LDRRMF with the MDRRM Plan to avoid the conduct of similar activities every budget year.

We recommend that the Management to prioritize the implementation of the development projects so that the expected output/outcome of the projects will be attained.  Also, to implement the projects by contract to avoid the difficulty in looking for the availability of the equipment.

We recommend that the management to ensure compliance with the above-cited regulation in appropriating and utilizing the 20% EDF. Also, to realign the remaining unimplemented maintenance and operating expenditures funded from the 20% EDF.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to require the contractor and the Municipal Engineer to submit a written explanation/justification on the above-noted delay implementation of the projects. Also, to consider the imposition of the appropriate remedies/sanctions provided in the above-cited regulation against the contractor.
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to ensure compliance with the above-cited regulation before approving the project to be implemented by administration.

We recommend that the concerned officials and employees to strictly comply with the above-mentioned regulation. Also, the management to identify the cause of the non-submission and institute the sanction provided in the penalty clause to the officials and employees who caused the non-submission of the required documents.

We recommend that the Management to evaluate the strategic location of the dumpsite by considering the factors surrounding the area and its long term effect. Also, to stop the operation of the open dumpsite for its garbage disposal and instead to use controlled dumpsite if sanitary landfill is not warranted considering the financial capability of the LGU.

We recommend that management in coordination with the barangay officials to establish MRF at the barangay level in compliance with the above-mentioned regulation. Also, to consider the design and layout of the MRF to accommodate the efficient segregation and collection of wastes at source as required by the IRR of RA 9003.

We recommend that Management and the Council to formulate/adopt a comprehensive plan on delinquency prevention which includes the following:
Local Code for Children
Local Development Plan for Children
Local Investment Plan for Children
Children’s Profile
Local State of Children’s Report and
The Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan.

We recommend that the Management to allocate one percent (1%) of its Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for the implementation of the LCPC PPAs in line with the adopted child delinquency prevention plan.

We recommend that:
The Local Chief Executive to allow only travel claims in line with the duties and functions of the officials and employees of the Municipality;
The department heads and other concerned officials to limit the claims for travelling expenses from the standpoint of necessity, economy and efficiency; and
The Local Finance Committee and the Committee on Appropriation to prioritize the expenditures to be funded.

We recommend that management refrain from authorizing deductions from the salaries of the employees that will reduce their net take home pay below 5,000.00 in CY 2018. Deductions from salaries should be prioritized as detailed in the GAA.
We recommend that management adhere to the provision of COA Circular 2017-001 by using a Certificate of Expenses Not Requiring Receipts as a supporting document in cases that the condition warrants.

We recommend that Management to require the Municipal Accountant to review the PPE schedule and determine the PPE due for depreciation. The corresponding depreciation expense shall be computed and booked up by drawing Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to come up with an accurate balance of the affected accounts.

Also, We recommend also, that the Accountant, before effecting payment on the claims, should see to it that all supporting documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers duly provided with relevant details.
