observations.reason: Reiterated in 2016 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management to require the Municipal Accountant to review the PPE schedule and determine the PPE due for depreciation. The corresponding depreciation expense shall be computed and booked up by drawing Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to come up with an accurate balance of the affected accounts.

We recommend that management to assess the present condition of these infrastructure assets and recognize it in the financial statement in accordance with PPSAS 17. Also, the Office of the Municipal Accountant to maintain a separate subsidiary ledger for each type/class of infrastructure assets.

We recommend that the management to strictly follow the guidelines on the Local Roads Asset Management System provided in the COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015 in accounting and reporting all its local roads and road network system.

We recommend that Management to require the Municipal Accountant to record the receipt and transfer of the motor vehicle in the books of account for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that Management to require the Municipal Treasurer to determine the basic RPT and SET and to submit to the Accounting Office a certified list of taxpayers with taxes due to ensure the recording of the same at the beginning of the year and ensure the accuracy of the related accounts.

We recommend that Management to impose stricter sanctions such as withholding of salaries or any amount due them in case of failure to comply pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 and 128 of PD No. 1445.
Also, to strictly enforce on the settlement of previously drawn cash advances. The prescribed period of settlement as provided for under existing rules and regulation to be strictly observed, otherwise, no new cash advance shall be granted to those who fail to comply.

We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant to exert more effort to reconcile the balance of reciprocal accounts to reflect correct balances of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements. Also, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile monthly their respective records to avoid the accumulation of the reconciling items and to facilitate the reconciliation of their balances.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the guidelines on the preparation and presentation of the Financial Statements specifically on the submission of a complete sets of financial statements.

We recommend that the Local Development Committee to identify and prioritize the development projects during the year so that intended beneficiaries of the municipality can immediately use the facilities and benefits that could be derived therefrom.

We recommend that Management require the MDRRMO to prioritize and implement the programs/projects/activities identified during the year to ensure the readiness of the Municipality in case of calamity.

We recommend that management invite the required observers to sit in the bidding proceedings as required under RA 9184 to achieve transparency and to provide a copy of the Notice of Invitation to observe the bidding process to COA.

We recommend that the BAC Secretariat submit the APP on the prescribed due date so that the PS-DBM can service their requirements for supplies and materials and equipment as reflected in the APP. Likewise, require the PS-DBM to issue CNAS if stocks to be procured were not available in the depot.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to effect immediately all reconciling items in the respective records to put in order the balances of accounts in the financial statements. Conduct periodic reconciliation of records in the ensuing periods to ensure the reliability of records and in compliance with Section 74 of PD 1445.
Also, to require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately deposit all undeposited collections as of December 31, 2016 to preclude misuse or misappropriation of the same.

We recommend that Management to insure the motor vehicles with the GSIS and register with the LTO to avoid additional expenses in terms of penalties and payment of damages for third party liabilities, in case of vehicular accidents.

We recommend that the designated GSO to account the handset units included in all the plan subscriptions paid by the LGU and issue the corresponding PAR or ARE to the accountable officer concerned.  Also, the accounting office to prepare the necessary journal entry to record the asset account and the possible adjustment to other affected accounts.
We recommend that Management limit the claims for travel and allow only when necessary and is most advantageous to the government from the standpoint of economy and efficiency.

We recommend that management:
Require the concerned officials to conduct inventory of the vehicles, determine the present running condition and evaluate if the costs of repair undertaken of the said vehicles had already surpassed the reasonable/economical amount;
Initiate the disposal of unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration due to natural elements and possible loss due to theft and loss of value and inform the auditor accordingly; and
Prepare and submit the journal entry voucher recording the reclassification of accounts from repair & maintenance account to the appropriate property account.

We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the affected accounts to the appropriate accounts in the ensuing year and strictly follow the updated description of accounts prescribed under Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 for proper accounting of the transactions.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record all purchases of supplies and materials to the appropriate asset accounts using the perpetual inventory method regardless of whether the items will be consumed within the accounting period or not.
Also, to require the property custodian of all requisitioning office to conduct physical inventory every semester and to submit a report to the GSO for consolidation, so that the cost of supplies and materials actually issued/consumed may be accurately determined and taken up in the books.

We recommend that Management require the Municipal Engineer to submit the Quarterly Reports on Government PPAs on the fifth (5th) day following the end of every quarter to avoid administrative disciplinary action as provided under Section 127 of PD 1445.

We reiterate our recommendation that Management ensure that notification to public and other form of announcements shall be made for PPA including the requirements in accordance with the above stated circular to further enhance transparency in government.

We recommend that Management require the GFPS to prepare the GAD AR, complete with the required information and includes accomplishments for PPAs attributed to GAD. Likewise, ensure that gender issues presented in the GPB are problems and concerns drawn from the gender analysis to properly identify the gender issues, the causes and the related objectives.

We recommend that management should institute action for the collection of the accounts on the active employees of the Municipality by sending them demand letters. If warranted, request for the write-off of the accounts as provided for in COA Circular No. 97-001.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant has regularly send letter of demand to all accountable Officers, even to those already separated with identified address deduction.
